questions.prompt: The completion of the transcontinental railroad in 1869 most directly contributed to which development?
choices: The growth of cattle ranching and national markets in the West
The end of slavery in the southern states
The annexation of the Hawaiian Islands
The passage of the Northwest Ordinance
questions.correctText: The growth of cattle ranching and national markets in the West

questions.prompt: Andrew Carnegie is most closely associated with which Gilded Age industry?
choices: Petroleum refining
Railroad transportation
Investment banking
Steel production
questions.correctText: Steel production

questions.prompt: John D. Rockefeller built his fortune primarily through which industry?
choices: Steel manufacturing
Oil refining
Textile mills
Coal mining
questions.correctText: Oil refining

questions.prompt: Which Gilded Age financier became famous for organizing trusts and reorganizing major railroads?
choices: Cornelius Vanderbilt
Andrew Carnegie
J. P. Morgan
Eugene V. Debs
questions.correctText: J. P. Morgan

questions.prompt: The Sherman Antitrust Act of 1890 was passed primarily to address concerns about
choices: the growth of labor unions
monopolies that restrained interstate trade
the influx of new immigrants
currency backed only by gold
questions.correctText: monopolies that restrained interstate trade

questions.prompt: Social Darwinism was most often used during the Gilded Age to justify
choices: the regulation of big business by the federal government
the unequal distribution of wealth in society
the expansion of public education
the breakup of large corporate trusts
questions.correctText: the unequal distribution of wealth in society

questions.prompt: Andrew Carnegie's 'Gospel of Wealth' argued that wealthy industrialists should
choices: redistribute their fortunes equally to all workers
use their wealth philanthropically for the public good
invest exclusively in foreign markets
refuse to pay any federal taxes
questions.correctText: use their wealth philanthropically for the public good

questions.prompt: The Knights of Labor differed from many later unions in that they
choices: accepted both skilled and unskilled workers as members
rejected the use of strikes under any circumstances
limited membership to native-born white men
opposed federal regulation of the workplace
questions.correctText: accepted both skilled and unskilled workers as members

questions.prompt: Samuel Gompers is best known as the founder and longtime leader of the
choices: Knights of Labor
Industrial Workers of the World
American Federation of Labor
Congress of Industrial Organizations
questions.correctText: American Federation of Labor

questions.prompt: The Haymarket Affair of 1886 in Chicago is most often cited as a turning point because it
choices: led directly to the passage of the Sherman Antitrust Act
associated organized labor with radicalism in the public mind
ended all use of injunctions against striking workers
produced the eight-hour workday by federal statute
questions.correctText: associated organized labor with radicalism in the public mind

questions.prompt: The Homestead Strike of 1892 took place at a plant owned by
choices: John D. Rockefeller
Andrew Carnegie
Cornelius Vanderbilt
George Pullman
questions.correctText: Andrew Carnegie

questions.prompt: President Grover Cleveland used federal troops to end which 1894 labor conflict?
choices: The Pullman Strike
The Haymarket Riot
The Homestead Strike
The Great Railroad Strike of 1877
questions.correctText: The Pullman Strike

questions.prompt: Between roughly 1880 and 1920, most immigrants entering the United States through Ellis Island came from
choices: Western and Northern Europe
Southern and Eastern Europe
East Asia and the Pacific
Latin America and the Caribbean
questions.correctText: Southern and Eastern Europe

questions.prompt: The Chinese Exclusion Act of 1882 is significant because it was the first federal law to
choices: restrict immigration on the basis of nationality
establish literacy tests for new arrivals
create a quota system for European nations
guarantee citizenship to all immigrants
questions.correctText: restrict immigration on the basis of nationality

questions.prompt: Nativist sentiment in the late nineteenth century was most often directed against
choices: Anglo-Saxon Protestant immigrants
Catholic and Jewish immigrants from Southern and Eastern Europe
African American migrants from the South
Native American peoples on the Plains
questions.correctText: Catholic and Jewish immigrants from Southern and Eastern Europe

questions.prompt: Jane Addams is best known for establishing
choices: Hull House, a settlement house in Chicago
the Women's Christian Temperance Union
the National American Woman Suffrage Association
the American Red Cross
questions.correctText: Hull House, a settlement house in Chicago

questions.prompt: Settlement houses of the Gilded Age primarily aimed to
choices: recruit immigrants for political machines
provide services and education to poor urban immigrants
deport unemployed laborers to their countries of origin
organize industrial workers into national unions
questions.correctText: provide services and education to poor urban immigrants

questions.prompt: The massacre at Wounded Knee in 1890 is generally regarded as
choices: the opening battle of the Plains Wars
the end of major armed Native American resistance on the Plains
a successful negotiation between the U.S. and the Lakota
the beginning of the reservation system
questions.correctText: the end of major armed Native American resistance on the Plains

questions.prompt: The Dawes Act of 1887 attempted to assimilate Native Americans by
choices: dividing tribal lands into individual family allotments
restoring large tribal reservations west of the Mississippi
guaranteeing dual citizenship for tribal members
creating bilingual federal schools on reservations
questions.correctText: dividing tribal lands into individual family allotments

questions.prompt: The Populist (People's) Party of the 1890s drew most of its support from
choices: industrial workers in northeastern cities
farmers in the South and West
wealthy bankers and railroad owners
African Americans in northern cities
questions.correctText: farmers in the South and West

questions.prompt: In his 1896 'Cross of Gold' speech, William Jennings Bryan argued for
choices: a return to a gold-only currency standard
the free and unlimited coinage of silver
the elimination of all paper money
an immediate end to the protective tariff
questions.correctText: the free and unlimited coinage of silver

questions.prompt: Muckrakers of the early 1900s are best described as
choices: business leaders who supported laissez-faire policies
journalists and writers who exposed social and economic abuses
Supreme Court justices who limited federal power
political bosses in major American cities
questions.correctText: journalists and writers who exposed social and economic abuses

questions.prompt: Upton Sinclair's novel 'The Jungle' helped lead directly to passage of the
choices: Sherman Antitrust Act
Meat Inspection Act and Pure Food and Drug Act
Federal Reserve Act
Clayton Antitrust Act
questions.correctText: Meat Inspection Act and Pure Food and Drug Act

questions.prompt: Ida Tarbell's most influential muckraking work exposed corruption in the
choices: meatpacking industry
Standard Oil Company
United States Senate
tenement housing of New York
questions.correctText: Standard Oil Company

questions.prompt: Jacob Riis's 'How the Other Half Lives' used photographs and reporting to draw attention to
choices: working conditions in coal mines
tenement life in New York City
child labor in southern textile mills
the abuse of Native Americans on reservations
questions.correctText: tenement life in New York City

questions.prompt: President Theodore Roosevelt's 'Square Deal' is most associated with
choices: isolating the United States from foreign trade
trust-busting, conservation, and consumer protection
restoring slavery in the western territories
ending all federal income taxes
questions.correctText: trust-busting, conservation, and consumer protection

questions.prompt: Theodore Roosevelt's conservation policies are best illustrated by his
choices: expansion of national parks and forest reserves
support for unlimited western land sales
opposition to all federal regulation of industry
promotion of unrestricted whaling and fur trapping
questions.correctText: expansion of national parks and forest reserves

questions.prompt: Both the Meat Inspection Act and the Pure Food and Drug Act of 1906 were intended primarily to
choices: protect American consumers from unsafe products
limit the political power of labor unions
support the rights of large food trusts
end federal regulation of interstate commerce
questions.correctText: protect American consumers from unsafe products

questions.prompt: President William Howard Taft's foreign policy of 'dollar diplomacy' encouraged
choices: American military occupation of European capitals
American investment as a tool of influence abroad
an alliance system with Russia and Japan
a complete withdrawal from Latin America
questions.correctText: American investment as a tool of influence abroad

questions.prompt: President Woodrow Wilson's 'New Freedom' program emphasized
choices: regulation of banks, tariffs, and trusts
imperial expansion in the Pacific
the unrestricted growth of corporate monopolies
the elimination of public education funding
questions.correctText: regulation of banks, tariffs, and trusts

questions.prompt: The Federal Reserve Act of 1913 created
choices: a national income tax for the first time
a central banking system to regulate currency and credit
the Securities and Exchange Commission
a permanent federal minimum wage
questions.correctText: a central banking system to regulate currency and credit

questions.prompt: The Clayton Antitrust Act of 1914 differed from the Sherman Antitrust Act by
choices: banning all labor unions in interstate industries
specifically exempting labor unions from antitrust prosecution
ending federal regulation of large corporations
creating the first federal income tax
questions.correctText: specifically exempting labor unions from antitrust prosecution

questions.prompt: The Nineteenth Amendment, ratified in 1920, accomplished which goal?
choices: It prohibited the manufacture and sale of alcohol.
It guaranteed women the right to vote in federal elections.
It established a federal income tax.
It allowed the direct election of United States senators.
questions.correctText: It guaranteed women the right to vote in federal elections.

questions.prompt: Prohibition in the United States was established by the
choices: Eighteenth Amendment in 1919
Sixteenth Amendment in 1913
Twenty-first Amendment in 1933
Fifteenth Amendment in 1870
questions.correctText: Eighteenth Amendment in 1919

questions.prompt: The Twenty-first Amendment, ratified in 1933, is unique because it
choices: extended voting rights to eighteen-year-olds
repealed a previous amendment to the Constitution
ended the poll tax in federal elections
limited the president to two elected terms
questions.correctText: repealed a previous amendment to the Constitution

questions.prompt: In Plessy v. Ferguson (1896), the Supreme Court upheld
choices: the constitutionality of 'separate but equal' racial segregation
the right of women to vote in state elections
the federal regulation of railroad rates
an outright ban on monopolistic trusts
questions.correctText: the constitutionality of 'separate but equal' racial segregation

questions.prompt: The NAACP, founded in 1909, was organized primarily to
choices: promote the colonization of African Americans in Africa
fight legal battles against racial discrimination
support the segregationist policies of southern states
represent industrial workers in collective bargaining
questions.correctText: fight legal battles against racial discrimination

questions.prompt: The explosion of the U.S.S. Maine in Havana harbor in 1898 helped lead directly to the
choices: War of 1812
Mexican-American War
Spanish-American War
World War I
questions.correctText: Spanish-American War

questions.prompt: 'Yellow journalism' during the 1890s is best defined as newspaper reporting that
choices: used sensationalism and exaggeration to attract readers
promoted strict neutrality in foreign affairs
focused mainly on agricultural and economic statistics
supported the Populist Party platform
questions.correctText: used sensationalism and exaggeration to attract readers

questions.prompt: As a result of the Spanish-American War, the United States gained control of all of the following except
choices: Puerto Rico
Guam
the Philippines
Mexico
questions.correctText: Mexico

questions.prompt: The annexation of Hawaii in 1898 was driven mainly by
choices: American sugar planters and strategic naval interests
the desire to end the slave trade in the Pacific
demands from Native Hawaiian leaders for U.S. statehood
fears of a German invasion of the Pacific
questions.correctText: American sugar planters and strategic naval interests

questions.prompt: Theodore Roosevelt's 'Roosevelt Corollary' to the Monroe Doctrine asserted that the United States could
choices: intervene in Latin American nations to maintain stability
annex any nation that defaulted on European loans
join European alliances during a major war
withdraw entirely from the Western Hemisphere
questions.correctText: intervene in Latin American nations to maintain stability

questions.prompt: The Panama Canal, completed in 1914, was significant primarily because it
choices: ended American involvement in Latin America
shortened the sea route between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans
guaranteed Panama's independence from Mexico
replaced the Erie Canal as the busiest U.S. waterway
questions.correctText: shortened the sea route between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans

questions.prompt: The Open Door Policy, promoted by Secretary of State John Hay, called for
choices: equal trading rights for all nations in China
the partition of China among European powers
the immediate withdrawal of all foreign troops from Asia
an alliance between the United States and Japan
questions.correctText: equal trading rights for all nations in China

questions.prompt: At the outbreak of World War I in 1914, President Woodrow Wilson initially declared a policy of
choices: immediate entry on the side of Great Britain
strict American neutrality
alliance with Germany and Austria-Hungary
intervention only in the Pacific theater
questions.correctText: strict American neutrality

questions.prompt: The sinking of the Lusitania in 1915 helped turn American public opinion against
choices: Germany
Great Britain
Mexico
Russia
questions.correctText: Germany

questions.prompt: The Zimmermann Telegram contributed to American entry into World War I because it
choices: promised Mexico the return of lost territory if it allied with Germany
revealed a secret British plan to invade Canada
exposed Russian plans to attack the United States
called for a ceasefire on all fronts
questions.correctText: promised Mexico the return of lost territory if it allied with Germany

questions.prompt: The United States formally entered World War I in
Wilson's Fourteen Points are best described as
choices: a plan for harsh punishment of the Central Powers
principles for a postwar peace, including a league of nations
a list of war aims for the British Empire
demands that ended the Russian Revolution
questions.correctText: principles for a postwar peace, including a league of nations

questions.prompt: The United States Senate ultimately rejected the Treaty of Versailles largely because of opposition to
choices: the partition of Germany
American membership in the League of Nations
free trade with Latin America
the redrawing of borders in Africa
questions.correctText: American membership in the League of Nations

questions.prompt: The Red Scare of 1919-1920 was driven mainly by fears of
choices: communist revolution and radical immigration
renewed warfare with Germany
the loss of women's suffrage
an invasion from Mexico
questions.correctText: communist revolution and radical immigration

questions.prompt: The trial and execution of Sacco and Vanzetti in the 1920s came to symbolize
choices: public concern about the abuse of immigrants and radicals
popular support for organized labor unions
the success of Prohibition enforcement
the achievements of women's suffrage activists
questions.correctText: public concern about the abuse of immigrants and radicals

questions.prompt: The Harlem Renaissance is best described as
choices: a flowering of African American art, music, and literature in the 1920s
a religious revival in rural New England
a federal urban renewal program of the 1930s
an immigration reform movement of the Progressive Era
questions.correctText: a flowering of African American art, music, and literature in the 1920s

questions.prompt: Langston Hughes is most often associated with which cultural movement?
choices: The Great Awakening
The Harlem Renaissance
Transcendentalism
The Lost Cause
questions.correctText: The Harlem Renaissance

questions.prompt: Louis Armstrong gained fame in the 1920s primarily as a leading figure in
choices: classical opera
jazz music
country and western swing
Tin Pan Alley songwriting
questions.correctText: jazz music

questions.prompt: The revival of the Ku Klux Klan in the 1920s differed from its Reconstruction-era predecessor in that it
choices: was strictly limited to the former Confederacy
targeted only African Americans
broadened its targets to include Catholics, Jews, and immigrants
officially supported women's suffrage
questions.correctText: broadened its targets to include Catholics, Jews, and immigrants

questions.prompt: The 1925 Scopes 'Monkey' Trial in Tennessee focused on the conflict between
choices: labor unions and corporate management
the federal government and the states
religious fundamentalism and modern science
Prohibition and the alcohol industry
questions.correctText: religious fundamentalism and modern science

questions.prompt: The 'flapper' image of the 1920s most directly reflected
choices: new freedoms and changing roles for some women
the spread of rural poverty
the success of Prohibition enforcement
growing support for socialism among the wealthy
questions.correctText: new freedoms and changing roles for some women

questions.prompt: Henry Ford's use of the moving assembly line is most significant because it
choices: made automobiles affordable to many ordinary Americans
encouraged a return to handcrafted production
reduced the demand for steel and rubber
ended the use of standardized parts
questions.correctText: made automobiles affordable to many ordinary Americans

questions.prompt: In the 1920s, 'buying on margin' refers to
choices: purchasing stocks with borrowed money
buying farms with federal subsidies
trading goods for foreign currency
selling government bonds to consumers
questions.correctText: purchasing stocks with borrowed money

questions.prompt: The stock market crash of October 29, 1929 is commonly known as
choices: Bloody Sunday
Black Tuesday
the Panic of 1893
the Long Depression
questions.correctText: Black Tuesday

questions.prompt: During the early 1930s, the term 'Hoovervilles' referred to
choices: shantytowns of homeless and unemployed Americans
federal relief camps for veterans
model housing built by President Hoover
luxury suburbs of major cities
questions.correctText: shantytowns of homeless and unemployed Americans

questions.prompt: Widespread bank failures in the early 1930s contributed to the Great Depression by
choices: expanding the money supply too rapidly
wiping out the savings of millions of depositors
encouraging foreign investment in the United States
reducing federal income taxes
questions.correctText: wiping out the savings of millions of depositors

questions.prompt: The Dust Bowl of the 1930s most directly affected
choices: coal-mining communities in Pennsylvania
farmers on the Great Plains
fishermen in New England
textile workers in New York City
questions.correctText: farmers on the Great Plains

questions.prompt: Franklin D. Roosevelt was first elected president in
President Franklin D. Roosevelt's 'fireside chats' were primarily intended to
choices: communicate directly with the American people by radio
negotiate trade agreements with European leaders
consult informally with Supreme Court justices
rally support among foreign heads of state
questions.correctText: communicate directly with the American people by radio

questions.prompt: The 'Hundred Days' at the start of Franklin D. Roosevelt's first term is remembered for
choices: a burst of major New Deal legislation
the entry of the United States into World War II
the impeachment of a sitting president
the elimination of the federal income tax
questions.correctText: a burst of major New Deal legislation

questions.prompt: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) provided
choices: jobs for young men in conservation and public works projects
loans for industrial corporations
armed protection of the U.S.-Mexico border
stock market regulation
questions.correctText: jobs for young men in conservation and public works projects

questions.prompt: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) was created during the New Deal to
choices: control flooding and bring electricity to a poor rural region
regulate the New York Stock Exchange
rebuild railroads damaged in World War I
extend voting rights to women in the South
questions.correctText: control flooding and bring electricity to a poor rural region

questions.prompt: The Works Progress Administration (WPA) is best known for
choices: paying farmers to reduce production
employing millions on public works and arts projects
providing pensions to retired industrial workers
supplying weapons to the Allies before World War II
questions.correctText: employing millions on public works and arts projects

questions.prompt: The Agricultural Adjustment Act (AAA) attempted to raise farm prices by
choices: paying farmers to reduce crop production
guaranteeing free transportation for all farm goods
abolishing the income tax on farm income
encouraging the export of all surplus crops
questions.correctText: paying farmers to reduce crop production

questions.prompt: The National Recovery Administration (NRA) sought to revive industry by
choices: establishing codes of fair competition for businesses
nationalizing all major banks
guaranteeing pensions for industrial workers
ending all federal regulation of business
questions.correctText: establishing codes of fair competition for businesses

questions.prompt: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) was created in 1934 mainly to
choices: regulate the stock market and prevent fraud
control immigration into urban areas
supply electricity to rural communities
oversee labor union elections
questions.correctText: regulate the stock market and prevent fraud

questions.prompt: The Social Security Act of 1935 established a federal program of
choices: national health insurance for all Americans
old-age pensions and unemployment insurance
free college education for veterans
agricultural price supports
questions.correctText: old-age pensions and unemployment insurance

questions.prompt: President Franklin D. Roosevelt's 1937 'court-packing' plan was an attempt to
choices: increase the number of justices on the Supreme Court
remove the Senate's role in approving treaties
merge the federal and state court systems
abolish the office of attorney general
questions.correctText: increase the number of justices on the Supreme Court

questions.prompt: The Wagner Act of 1935 most directly affected
choices: the right of workers to form unions and bargain collectively
the federal income tax on corporations
voting rights for African Americans
the breakup of large oil companies
questions.correctText: the right of workers to form unions and bargain collectively

questions.prompt: Eleanor Roosevelt is best remembered during the New Deal era for her work as
choices: the first female Supreme Court justice
an outspoken advocate for civil rights, women, and the poor
the head of the Manhattan Project
the founder of the United Nations
questions.correctText: an outspoken advocate for civil rights, women, and the poor

questions.prompt: By the late 1930s, the rise of totalitarianism in Europe and Asia was best illustrated by
choices: constitutional monarchies in Britain and France
fascist and militarist regimes in Germany, Italy, and Japan
the spread of liberal democracy in Eastern Europe
the unification of Latin American republics
questions.correctText: fascist and militarist regimes in Germany, Italy, and Japan

questions.prompt: American neutrality acts of the mid-1930s were intended primarily to
choices: keep the United States out of future European wars
increase military aid to Britain and France
build a large standing army
support the League of Nations
questions.correctText: keep the United States out of future European wars

questions.prompt: The Lend-Lease Act of 1941 allowed the United States to
choices: provide weapons and supplies to nations fighting the Axis powers
send American troops into Eastern Europe
join the League of Nations
occupy parts of Latin America
questions.correctText: provide weapons and supplies to nations fighting the Axis powers

questions.prompt: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor occurred on
choices: September 1, 1939
December 7, 1941
June 6, 1944
August 6, 1945
questions.correctText: December 7, 1941

questions.prompt: Franklin D. Roosevelt described Pearl Harbor as
choices: 'a day that will live in infamy'
'the new birth of freedom'
'the splendid little war'
'the war to end all wars'
questions.correctText: 'a day that will live in infamy'

questions.prompt: During World War II, the term 'arsenal of democracy' referred to the role of the United States in
choices: supplying weapons and equipment to the Allies
exporting democratic ideas to Latin America
training only African American soldiers
negotiating peace in the Pacific
questions.correctText: supplying weapons and equipment to the Allies

questions.prompt: Executive Order 9066, signed by President Franklin D. Roosevelt in 1942, led to
choices: the internment of Japanese Americans on the West Coast
the integration of the U.S. armed forces
the dropping of the atomic bomb on Japan
the creation of the Manhattan Project
questions.correctText: the internment of Japanese Americans on the West Coast

questions.prompt: Korematsu v. United States (1944) is most often cited as an example of
choices: the Supreme Court upholding the wartime internment of Japanese Americans
the Court striking down racial segregation in schools
the Court protecting the free speech of socialists
the Court limiting the power of the president
questions.correctText: the Supreme Court upholding the wartime internment of Japanese Americans

questions.prompt: 'Rosie the Riveter' became a wartime symbol of
choices: women working in defense industries during World War II
African American soldiers in the Pacific
isolationist mothers protesting the war
wartime rationing of consumer goods
questions.correctText: women working in defense industries during World War II

questions.prompt: The Tuskegee Airmen are remembered as
choices: African American pilots who served with distinction in World War II
Native American code talkers in the Pacific
Japanese American soldiers in Europe
women pilots ferrying planes within the United States
questions.correctText: African American pilots who served with distinction in World War II

questions.prompt: Navajo Code Talkers contributed to victory in World War II by
choices: transmitting secure military communications using their language
designing the first jet aircraft
leading the invasion of North Africa
negotiating Japan's surrender
questions.correctText: transmitting secure military communications using their language

questions.prompt: The Manhattan Project was a secret World War II effort to
choices: develop the atomic bomb
break the Japanese naval code
design long-range bombers
build the Panama Canal
questions.correctText: develop the atomic bomb

questions.prompt: The American strategy of 'island hopping' in the Pacific aimed to
choices: bypass heavily defended Japanese islands and capture key strategic ones
occupy every Japanese-held island in turn
avoid direct engagement with the Japanese navy
establish permanent colonies in the Pacific
questions.correctText: bypass heavily defended Japanese islands and capture key strategic ones

questions.prompt: The Battle of Midway in 1942 is considered a turning point because it
choices: destroyed much of Japan's carrier fleet in the Pacific
ended Italian participation in the war
led directly to D-Day
forced Germany to surrender
questions.correctText: destroyed much of Japan's carrier fleet in the Pacific

questions.prompt: Allied forces first opened a major land front against the Axis powers in
choices: the Soviet Union in 1941
North Africa in 1942
France in 1944
the Philippines in 1942
questions.correctText: North Africa in 1942

questions.prompt: The Allied invasion of Italy in 1943 led most directly to
choices: the fall of Mussolini and Italian surrender
the entry of Japan into the war
the immediate liberation of France
the end of Lend-Lease
questions.correctText: the fall of Mussolini and Italian surrender

questions.prompt: D-Day, the Allied invasion of Normandy, took place on
choices: June 6, 1944
December 7, 1941
August 15, 1945
questions.correctText: June 6, 1944

questions.prompt: The Battle of the Bulge in late 1944 was significant because it
choices: was Germany's last major offensive on the Western Front
marked the entry of the Soviet Union into the war
ended Allied bombing of German cities
led to Japan's immediate surrender
questions.correctText: was Germany's last major offensive on the Western Front

questions.prompt: V-E Day, marking victory in Europe, is observed on
choices: June 6, 1944
August 15, 1945
December 7, 1941

questions.prompt: President Harry S. Truman's decision to drop atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki in August 1945 was justified primarily as a way to
choices: force Japan to surrender without an Allied invasion of the home islands
punish Japan for the bombing of London
demonstrate American power to Latin American nations
end the war in Europe
questions.correctText: force Japan to surrender without an Allied invasion of the home islands

questions.prompt: V-J Day refers to the date of
choices: Japan's surrender, ending World War II
the German invasion of Poland
the firing on Fort Sumter
the signing of the Treaty of Versailles
questions.correctText: Japan's surrender, ending World War II

questions.prompt: The GI Bill of 1944 most directly provided returning World War II veterans with
choices: benefits for education, home loans, and unemployment
free passage to overseas colonies
automatic admission to the U.S. Senate
exemption from all federal taxes
questions.correctText: benefits for education, home loans, and unemployment

questions.prompt: Taken together, the alphabet agencies of the New Deal best illustrate
choices: a dramatic expansion of the federal government's role in the economy
the triumph of laissez-faire economics
a return to nineteenth-century gold-standard policies
the rejection of all government planning
questions.correctText: a dramatic expansion of the federal government's role in the economy
